description: Theory underlying the existence, organization, and applications of computers. Strong transfer pathway to BS programs at CUNY senior colleges.

requirement_groups.name: Common Core - Math (STEM variant)
requirement_groups.rule: Take MAT 301 (satisfies STEM Common Core + major req)

requirement_groups.name: Common Core - Life and Physical Sciences
requirement_groups.rule: Take PHY 215 (calculus-based physics, also satisfies major)

requirement_groups.name: Flexible Core
requirement_groups.rule: 1 course from each of 5 Flex areas + 1 additional. Max 2 per discipline.

requirement_groups.name: Major - Required Math
requirement_groups.rule: Take MAT 206 or MAT 301 (whichever not yet taken)

flexible_core.rule: One course from each of the 5 areas + one additional from any. Max 2 courses per discipline.

ENG 101.title: English Composition I
ENG 101.description: Introduction to college-level writing. Students learn to compose academic essays with effective thesis statements, organization, evidence, and clarity. Includes critical reading and revision.

ENG 201.title: Introduction to Literature
ENG 201.description: Continuation of ENG 101 with focus on literary analysis. Students read and write about fiction, poetry, and drama while developing research and argumentative writing skills.

MAT 100.title: Fundamentals of Mathematics I
MAT 100.description: Whole numbers, fractions, decimals, percentages, ratios, and an introduction to algebra. Designed to satisfy the MQR Common Core requirement for non-STEM majors.

MAT 104.title: Algebra & Trigonometry / Quantitative Reasoning
MAT 104.description: Algebra topics including functions, exponents, logarithms, and trigonometry. Required for nursing and several health programs.

MAT 150.title: Introduction to Statistics
MAT 150.description: Descriptive statistics, probability, sampling, hypothesis testing, correlation, and regression. Common math choice for social-science and health majors.

MAT 161.5.title: Mathematics Literacy & Quantitative Reasoning
MAT 161.5.description: Co-requisite quantitative reasoning course covering numeracy, proportional reasoning, algebraic reasoning, and basic statistics for non-STEM majors.

MAT 206.title: Pre-Calculus
MAT 206.description: Functions, polynomial and rational functions, exponential and logarithmic functions, trigonometry, conic sections. Prepares students for calculus.

MAT 301.title: Analytic Geometry & Calculus I
MAT 301.description: Limits, continuity, derivatives, differentiation rules, applications of derivatives, and an introduction to integration.

CSC 101.title: Computers and Society
CSC 101.description: Survey of computer applications and their social impact. Hardware, software, networking, and ethical issues in computing.

CSC 103.title: Programming Logic
CSC 103.description: Problem solving and algorithmic thinking. Pseudocode, flowcharts, and structured programming concepts.

CSC 111.title: Introduction to Programming
CSC 111.description: Fundamentals of programming including algorithms, problem solving, data types, loops, conditionals, modular programming, pointers, arrays, strings, basic file processing, structures and simple classes.

CSC 211.title: Object-Oriented Programming
CSC 211.description: Object-oriented programming concepts: classes, inheritance, polymorphism, encapsulation, exception handling, GUI programming.

CSC 231.title: Discrete Mathematics for Computer Science
CSC 231.description: Logic, set theory, functions, relations, algorithms, recursion, recurrence relations, counting, graph theory, trees, Boolean algebra.

CSC 232.title: Data Structures
CSC 232.description: Linear and non-linear data structures: stacks, queues, linked lists, hash tables, trees, graphs. Searching and sorting algorithms with asymptotic analysis.

CSC 203.title: Software Development
CSC 203.description: Software development life cycle, OO paradigm, design patterns, event-driven programming, team-based application development.

CIS 100.title: Introduction to Computer Applications
CIS 100.description: Introduction to PC operating systems, word processing, spreadsheets, presentation software, and the Internet.

CIS 165.title: Introduction to Computer Information Systems
CIS 165.description: Computer concepts, hardware, software, networking, databases, and information systems in business.

CIS 272.title: Internet Programming
CIS 272.description: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, server-side scripting, and web application development.

CIS 285.title: Database Management Systems
CIS 285.description: Relational database design, SQL, normalization, transactions, and modern DBMS concepts.

CIS 316.title: Cybersecurity Fundamentals
CIS 316.description: Foundations of cybersecurity: threats, cryptography, network security, identity management.

CIS 317.title: Network Security
CIS 317.description: Network attacks and defenses, firewalls, intrusion detection, VPNs, and secure protocols.

CIS 345.title: Computer Networks
CIS 345.description: OSI and TCP/IP models, routing, switching, network architecture, and protocols.

CIS 359.title: Mobile Application Development
CIS 359.description: Designing and building applications for iOS and Android platforms.

CIS 362.title: Operating Systems
CIS 362.description: OS concepts including processes, threads, memory management, file systems, and concurrency.

CIS 364.title: Cloud Computing
CIS 364.description: Cloud architectures, services, deployment models, and hands-on work with AWS/Azure.

CIS 385.title: Advanced Database Systems
CIS 385.description: Advanced SQL, query optimization, NoSQL, distributed databases.

CIS 395.title: Data Mining / Analytics
CIS 395.description: Concepts and tools of data mining, machine-learning fundamentals, and business analytics.

GIS 201.title: Introduction to Geographic Information Systems
GIS 201.description: Spatial data, mapping, and analysis using GIS software.

BIO 425.title: Anatomy & Physiology I
BIO 425.description: Structure and function of the human body: cells, tissues, integumentary, skeletal, muscular, and nervous systems. Required for nursing.

BIO 110.title: General Biology I
BIO 110.description: Cell biology, biochemistry, genetics, and molecular biology with laboratory.

BIO 210.title: General Biology II
BIO 210.description: Evolution, ecology, biodiversity, plant and animal physiology with laboratory.

CHE 121.title: Fundamentals of General Chemistry I
CHE 121.description: Atomic structure, bonding, stoichiometry, gas laws, and solutions. Required for nursing.

CHE 201.title: General Chemistry I
CHE 201.description: Stoichiometry, atomic structure, periodic properties, bonding, gases, thermochemistry.

CHE 202.title: General Chemistry II
CHE 202.description: Solutions, kinetics, equilibrium, acid-base, thermodynamics, electrochemistry.

PHY 215.title: University Physics I
PHY 215.description: Calculus-based physics: kinematics, dynamics, energy, momentum, rotation, oscillations, waves.

PHY 225.title: University Physics II
PHY 225.description: Calculus-based physics: electricity, magnetism, optics, modern physics intro.

PSY 100.title: Introduction to Psychology
PSY 100.description: Survey of psychology: biological bases, learning, memory, cognition, development, personality, abnormal psychology, social psychology.

SOC 100.title: Introduction to Sociology
SOC 100.description: Social structure, culture, socialization, deviance, stratification, institutions.

ANT 100.title: Introduction to Anthropology
ANT 100.description: Survey of physical, cultural, and archaeological anthropology.

ECO 201.title: Macroeconomics
ECO 201.description: National income, employment, money, banking, fiscal and monetary policy.

POL 100.title: American Government
POL 100.description: Constitution, federalism, branches of government, civil rights, political parties, elections.

HIS 101.title: United States History I
HIS 101.description: U.S. history from colonial era to Reconstruction.

HIS 102.title: United States History II
HIS 102.description: U.S. history from Reconstruction to the present.

HIS 122.title: World History II
HIS 122.description: World civilizations from 1500 to the present.

SPE 100.title: Fundamentals of Public Speaking
SPE 100.description: Theory and practice of public speaking, including organization, delivery, and audience analysis.

ART 100.title: Introduction to Art History
ART 100.description: Survey of major movements in Western and non-Western art.

MUS 100.title: Introduction to Music
MUS 100.description: Survey of music styles and history.

THE 100.title: Introduction to Theatre
THE 100.description: Theatre history, dramatic literature, and production elements.

PHI 100.title: Introduction to Philosophy
PHI 100.description: Major philosophical questions: knowledge, reality, ethics, freedom.

SPN 100.title: Elementary Spanish I
SPN 100.description: Basic vocabulary, grammar, conversation, and reading.

ETH 100.title: Introduction to Ethnic Studies
ETH 100.description: Race, ethnicity, identity, and inequality in the U.S.

WGS 100.title: Introduction to Women's and Gender Studies
WGS 100.description: Gender as social construction; intersectionality; feminist movements.
